title: What is Python's __init_subclass__ method?
options: Called when a class is subclassed; receives the subclass and any keyword args from the class statement. Useful for registering subclasses, enforcing constraints, or implementing plugin systems without metaclasses
correct_answer: Called when a class is subclassed; receives the subclass and any keyword args from the class statement. Useful for registering subclasses, enforcing constraints, or implementing plugin systems without metaclasses

title: What is the difference between map, filter, and reduce in Python?
options: map(fn, iterable) transforms each element; filter(fn, iterable) keeps elements where fn is truthy; reduce(fn, iterable) accumulates to single value (from functools). All return iterators (except reduce returns a value)
They all do the same thing
correct_answer: map(fn, iterable) transforms each element; filter(fn, iterable) keeps elements where fn is truthy; reduce(fn, iterable) accumulates to single value (from functools). All return iterators (except reduce returns a value)

title: What is Python's enum module?
description: How do you create enumerations in Python?
options: class Color(Enum): RED=1; GREEN=2; BLUE=3 creates named constants. Members are singletons; access via Color.RED or Color(1). IntEnum for int compatibility; Flag for bitwise ops; StrEnum (3.11) for string values. Prevents invalid values

title: Implement a Python decorator that measures and logs function execution time.
description: Create a timing decorator with logging.
correct_answer:
import time
import functools
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def timed(func):
    @functools.wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        start = time.perf_counter()
        try:
            result = func(*args, **kwargs)
            return result
        finally:
            elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
            logger.info(f'{func.__name__} took {elapsed:.4f}s')
    return wrapper

# @timed
# def slow_function(): time.sleep(1)

title: What is the difference between threading.Lock and threading.RLock?
description: When to use RLock over Lock?
options: Lock can only be acquired once; blocks if same thread tries to re-acquire (deadlock risk). RLock (reentrant lock) allows the same thread to acquire multiple times; must release same number of times. Use RLock when a locked function calls another locked function
correct_answer: Lock can only be acquired once; blocks if same thread tries to re-acquire (deadlock risk). RLock (reentrant lock) allows the same thread to acquire multiple times; must release same number of times. Use RLock when a locked function calls another locked function
